    <h1 {...{
      "id": "listening-to-inbox-events"
    }}>{`Listening to Inbox Events`}</h1>
    <p>{`This tutorial explains how to listen to events from the Inbox in your application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Messaging extension provides ways to monitor:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Inbox-Level Events`}</strong>{`: Track inbox display events using `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Inbox State Changes`}</strong>{`: Track loading, success, and error states of the inbox container`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Content Card Events`}</strong>{`: Listen to user interactions with individual cards within the inbox`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "inbox-level-events"
    }}>{`Inbox-Level Events`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AepInbox`}</inlineCode>{` composable requires an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AepInboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` to handle inbox-level events such as when the inbox is displayed. The Messaging extension provides `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` which implements this interface and automatically tracks inbox display events.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "using-inboxeventobserver"
    }}>{`Using InboxEventObserver`}</h3>
    <p>{`Create an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` in your ViewModel with a reference to the provider:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.InboxEventObserver
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.MessagingInboxProvider
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.Surface
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.SharingStarted
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.stateIn

class InboxViewModel : ViewModel() {
    val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(Surface("inbox"))
    
    val observer = InboxEventObserver(inboxProvider)

    val inboxUIState = inboxProvider.getInboxUI()
        .stateIn(
            scope = viewModelScope,
            started = SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000),
            initialValue = InboxUIState.Loading
        )
}

@Composable
fun InboxScreen(viewModel: InboxViewModel) {
    val inboxUIState by viewModel.inboxUIState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()

    AepInbox(
        uiState = inboxUIState,
        inboxStyle = InboxUIStyle.Builder().build(),
        observer = viewModel.observer
    )
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` automatically:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Tracks inbox display events when the inbox is shown`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Prevents duplicate display events for the same inbox state`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Sends tracking data to Adobe Journey Optimizer`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Survives configuration changes (screen rotation, theme changes, etc.)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "inbox-state-changes"
    }}>{`Inbox State Changes`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUIState`}</inlineCode>{` sealed interface represents the different states of the inbox. By collecting the state flow from `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`MessagingInboxProvider`}</inlineCode>{`, you can respond to state transitions.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "inboxuistate-types"
    }}>{`InboxUIState Types`}</h3>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`State`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Description`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`InboxUIState.Loading`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`The inbox is fetching content from the device cache`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`InboxUIState.Success`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Content loaded successfully (may contain cards or be empty)`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`InboxUIState.Error`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`An error occurred while loading content`}</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "observing-state-changes"
    }}>{`Observing State Changes`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use the Flow returned by `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInboxUI()`}</inlineCode>{` to observe state changes:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-1"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`class InboxViewModel : ViewModel() {
    val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(Surface("inbox"))
    
    val observer = InboxEventObserver(inboxProvider)

    val inboxUIState: StateFlow<InboxUIState> = inboxProvider.getInboxUI()
        .stateIn(
            scope = viewModelScope,
            started = SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000),
            initialValue = InboxUIState.Loading
        )
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "responding-to-state-changes-in-ui"
    }}>{`Responding to State Changes in UI`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can respond to state changes directly in your composable:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-2"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`@Composable
fun InboxScreen(viewModel: InboxViewModel) {
    val inboxUIState by viewModel.inboxUIState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()

    // Respond to state changes
    LaunchedEffect(inboxUIState) {
        when (inboxUIState) {
            is InboxUIState.Loading -> {
                Log.d("Inbox", "Loading inbox...")
            }
            is InboxUIState.Success -> {
                val successState = inboxUIState as InboxUIState.Success
                Log.d("Inbox", "Loaded \${successState.items.size} cards")
            }
            is InboxUIState.Error -> {
                val errorState = inboxUIState as InboxUIState.Error
                Log.e("Inbox", "Error: \${errorState.error.message}")
            }
        }
    }

    AepInbox(
        uiState = inboxUIState,
        inboxStyle = InboxUIStyle.Builder().build(),
        observer = viewModel.observer
    )
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "accessing-success-state-data"
    }}>{`Accessing Success State Data`}</h3>
    <p>{`When the inbox loads successfully, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUIState.Success`}</inlineCode>{` contains:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`template`}</inlineCode>{`: The inbox template with heading, layout, and styling information`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`items`}</inlineCode>{`: List of `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`AepUI`}</inlineCode>{` objects representing the content cards`}</li>
    </ul>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-3"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`when (val state = inboxUIState) {
    is InboxUIState.Success -> {
        val cardCount = state.items.size
        val heading = state.template.heading
        val layout = state.template.layout
        
        // Check if inbox is empty
        if (state.items.isEmpty()) {
            Log.d("Inbox", "Inbox is empty")
        }
    }
    // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "handling-errors"
    }}>{`Handling Errors`}</h3>
    <p>{`When an error occurs, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUIState.Error`}</inlineCode>{` contains the throwable with error details:`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-4"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`when (val state = inboxUIState) {
    is InboxUIState.Error -> {
        val errorMessage = state.error.message ?: "Unknown error"
        
        // Show error UI or retry
        showErrorDialog(errorMessage) {
            viewModel.refresh()
        }
    }
    // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "content-card-events"
    }}>{`Content Card Events`}</h2>
    <p>{`Individual content cards within the inbox emit events when users interact with them. To listen to these events:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Implement a `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ContentCardUIEventListener`}</inlineCode>{` to handle card events`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Wrap it in a `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ContentCardEventObserver`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Pass the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ContentCardEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` to `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` in your ViewModel`}</li>
    </ol>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example"
    }}>{`Example`}</h3>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-5"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.aepcomposeui.AepUI
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.ContentCardEventObserver
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.ContentCardUIEventListener
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.InboxEventObserver
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.MessagingInboxProvider
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.messaging.Surface
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.SharingStarted
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.stateIn

// In ViewModel - observer survives configuration changes
class InboxViewModel : ViewModel() {
    private val cardCallback = object : ContentCardUIEventListener {
        override fun onDisplay(aepUI: AepUI<*, *>) {
            // Handle card display
        }
        
        override fun onInteract(
            aepUI: AepUI<*, *>,
            interactionId: String?,
            actionUrl: String?
        ): Boolean {
            // Handle card interaction
            return false
        }
        
        override fun onDismiss(aepUI: AepUI<*, *>) {
            // Handle card dismissal
        }
    }
    
    val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(Surface("inbox"))
    
    val observer = InboxEventObserver(
        inboxProvider,
        ContentCardEventObserver(cardCallback)
    )

    val inboxUIState = inboxProvider.getInboxUI()
        .stateIn(
            scope = viewModelScope,
            started = SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(5000),
            initialValue = InboxUIState.Loading
        )
}

@Composable
fun InboxScreen(viewModel: InboxViewModel) {
    val inboxUIState by viewModel.inboxUIState.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()

    AepInbox(
        uiState = inboxUIState,
        inboxStyle = InboxUIStyle.Builder().build(),
        observer = viewModel.observer
    )
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`For detailed information on content card events, implementing `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ContentCardUIEventListener`}</inlineCode>{`, and handling actionable URLs, see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../content-card-ui/Android/tutorial/listening-content-card-events.md"
      }}>{`Listening to Content Card Events`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Create Observer in ViewModel`}</strong>{`: Always create the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`InboxEventObserver`}</inlineCode>{` in your ViewModel with a reference to the provider. This ensures the observer survives configuration changes (screen rotation, theme changes, etc.):`}</li>
    </ol>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="1" languages="Kotlin" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "kotlin-6"
    }}>{`Kotlin`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`class InboxViewModel : ViewModel() {
    val inboxProvider = MessagingInboxProvider(Surface("inbox"))
    val observer = InboxEventObserver(inboxProvider)
}
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 2
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Avoid Heavy Work in Event Handlers`}</strong>{`: Event handlers are called on the main thread. Keep them lightweight and dispatch heavy work to background threads.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Handle Errors Gracefully`}</strong>{`: Provide user-friendly error messages and retry options when the inbox fails to load.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Use Lifecycle-aware Collection`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`collectAsStateWithLifecycle()`}</inlineCode>{` to automatically stop collecting when the UI is not visible.`}</p>
      </li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
